title: Procedural paint rendering primitives
short_description: Implement the signature look — noise-displaced wobbly strokes with variable width/jitter, paint/paper texture fills, paint-reveal draw-on animation, and glyph-outline-tracing text strokes — golden-tested at parameter extremes with property-tested wobble decorrelation across node IDs.
long_description_md:
# Procedural paint rendering primitives

## Why this task exists

Capability 4 in the product spec, the thing that makes anima-ai look like anima-ai instead of a generic vector-shape renderer. Feasibility research named this the differentiator moment: noise-displaced wobbly strokes, paint/paper texture fills, hand-drawn imperfection, and proper glyph-outline text stroking (Doodly's documented craft failure). Everything here must be a pure function of `(project_seed, node_id, t)` — no wall-clock, no ambient `random`, no dict-iteration-order dependence — because this is the pixel layer the determinism contract and the golden-image trust story rest on. Style-pack authors (the `crude` pack, later `flat`) consume these primitives as parameters, not forks; this task ships the primitives, not the packs.

## Roadmap fit

First task in **Phase 3: Paint rendering + `crude` style pack**. It depends on:

- `seeded-rng-service` — the `SeededRNG(project_seed).for_node(node_id)` stream contract every wobble/jitter/texture-placement decision must draw from. No other randomness source is permitted anywhere in this task's code paths.
- `skia-rasterizer-cache` — the Skia rasterization + static-layer-cache pipeline this task's primitives render into. Paint primitives are Skia path/shader/filter constructions consumed by that pipeline; this task does not build the cache or the frame-streaming pipeline itself.
- `golden-image-harness` — the addressable `GoldenBaseline` registry, vendored YIQ/AA-aware comparator, and pinned-container generate/compare/update workflow this task's fixtures register against. This task does not build the harness, only uses it.

Downstream, `style-pack-system` defines the typed `stroke_profile`/`textures` schema fields packs use to parameterize these primitives, and `crude-pack` is the first pack to actually invoke them at content scale (12+ characters, 20+ props). This task ships the primitive implementations and their own golden coverage at parameter extremes — not pack content.

## Required contract and scope

Implement, under a dedicated internal module (e.g. `anima/_internal/paint/`), four primitive families, each a pure function of stable inputs and each independently golden-tested:

### 1. Wobbly stroke paths

Given an input path (line/polyline/curve/shape outline) and a stroke profile (amplitude, frequency, width-variation range, jitter magnitude — the same fields `style-pack-system`'s `stroke_profile` schema will pin), produce a noise-displaced Skia path: sample the input path, displace each sample perpendicular to the tangent using a deterministic noise function seeded from the node's RNG stream, vary stroke width along the path length within the configured range, and add small positional jitter to segment endpoints. Must handle straight lines, curves, and closed shapes (outlines) without self-intersection artifacts at reasonable amplitude values, and must degrade gracefully (near-identity output) at amplitude 0.

### 2. Paint/paper texture fills

Procedural, deterministic texture generation (paper grain, dry-brush/paint-edge mottling) applicable as a fill or overlay on shapes and backgrounds, parameterized by scale, opacity, and a seed-derived offset so two same-texture fills on different nodes don't tile identically. Must render at whatever the requested resolution/DPI is without visible repeating artifacts at typical scene sizes, and must be a Skia shader or pre-rasterized deterministic pattern — no runtime image download, no PIL/numpy noise sourced from `numpy.random` (must route through `SeededRNG`).

### 3. Paint-reveal draw-on animation

An animation primitive (referenced by the product spec's `paint-reveal` enter/exit family) that progressively reveals a wobbly stroke path as if being drawn/painted on, driven purely by animation progress `t ∈ [0, 1]` — not wall-clock. Must produce a stable, monotonic reveal (no flicker/jitter in what's already revealed as `t` advances) and integrate with the existing animation system's easing/keyframe evaluation rather than inventing a second animation mechanism. This task implements the paint-reveal rasterization primitive; wiring it as a selectable enter/exit animation choice in scene-authoring code is the animation system's/renderer's integration surface — confirm the hookup exists and is exercised by a fixture, but do not redesign the animation system's public API here.

### 4. Glyph-outline-tracing text strokes

Proper text stroking that traces each glyph's actual outline path (via Skia's glyph-to-path conversion) and applies the same wobbly-stroke primitive from (1) to each glyph outline, rather than applying a naive uniform outline-stroke to the whole text run (the specific failure mode the product spec calls out in Doodly). Must work across the bundled font set for at least the ASCII/Latin-1 glyph range, preserve legibility at stroke amplitudes intended for on-screen text (tighter defaults than shape strokes), and remain deterministic per `(project_seed, node_id, glyph_index)` so re-renders are pixel-identical and adjacent glyphs don't wobble in lockstep.

## Determinism requirements (non-negotiable, shared across all four)

- Every stochastic decision (noise seed offsets, jitter samples, texture placement offsets, per-glyph substreams) must derive from `SeededRNG(project_seed).for_node(node_id)`, using additional deterministic sub-keying (e.g. glyph index, segment index) documented and stable — not a second ad-hoc RNG source.
- Same `(project_seed, node_id)` re-rendered twice must produce byte-identical rasterized output.
- Two different node IDs with identical stroke-profile parameters must not produce visually correlated wobble (no shared phase/offset) — this is the property-tested decorrelation requirement.
- No `random`, `numpy.random`, `time`, `os.urandom`, or hash-randomization-sensitive code path anywhere in `anima/_internal/paint/`.

## Golden coverage requirement

Register golden baselines (via `golden-image-harness`'s registry) at parameter extremes for each primitive family: minimum and maximum wobble amplitude/frequency, minimum and maximum stroke-width variation, zero and maximum texture opacity/scale, paint-reveal at `t=0`, `t=0.5`, `t=1.0`, and glyph-stroke at small and large point sizes. Extremes matter more than the middle of the range here: this is where self-intersection, texture tiling, and glyph-outline degeneracies actually show up. Canonical baselines are generated only in the pinned container per the harness's existing policy; this task must not bless its own baselines outside that container.

## Explicitly out of scope

- The `skia-rasterizer-cache` pipeline itself (frame streaming, static-layer caching, PyAV handoff) — this task's primitives are consumed by that pipeline, not authors of it.
- `style-pack-system`'s `StylePack` schema, pack pinning/resolution, `anima assets`, and `STYLE.PALETTE_VIOLATION` — separate task; this task's primitives accept plain stroke/texture parameters, not resolved pack objects.
- The `crude` pack's actual content — characters, props, blob-nation shapes, label-pop animation set, palette, typography, license vetting. That is `crude-pack`'s job, built on top of these primitives.
- Map-specific animations (territory fill sweep, border morph, arrow growth) — Phase 4 map engine scope, even though they may reuse the wobbly-stroke primitive internally later.
- The animation system's public enter/exit API surface and timeline compiler wiring beyond confirming paint-reveal is reachable and fixture-tested.
- Golden-image harness infrastructure (container, comparator, registry/CLI commands) — consumed here, not built here.

deliverables.description: Implement the wobbly-stroke primitive (`anima/_internal/paint/stroke.py`): noise-displaced Skia path generation from an input path + stroke profile (amplitude, frequency, width variation, jitter), all randomness routed through `SeededRNG.for_node`, handling straight/curved/closed paths without self-intersection artifacts at documented amplitude ranges and degrading to near-identity at amplitude 0.

verification.command: ruff check src/anima/_internal/paint/stroke.py tests/paint/test_stroke.py && mypy --strict src/anima/_internal/paint/stroke.py && pytest tests/paint/test_stroke.py -q

deliverables.description: Implement paint/paper texture fills (`anima/_internal/paint/texture.py`) and paint-reveal draw-on animation (`anima/_internal/paint/reveal.py`): deterministic seed-derived texture generation with non-repeating placement across nodes, and monotonic non-flickering progressive stroke reveal driven purely by animation progress t, integrated with the existing animation system's keyframe/easing evaluation rather than a parallel mechanism.

Implement glyph-outline-tracing text strokes (`anima/_internal/paint/text_stroke.py`): convert glyphs to Skia paths via glyph-to-path conversion and apply the wobbly-stroke primitive per glyph outline (not a uniform whole-run outline), deterministic per (project_seed, node_id, glyph_index), legible at on-screen text stroke amplitudes across the bundled ASCII/Latin-1 glyph range.

Property-test decorrelation and re-render determinism across all four primitive families with Hypothesis: same (project_seed, node_id) renders byte-identical output across two runs; distinct node IDs with identical stroke-profile parameters produce decorrelated wobble (no shared phase/offset detectable via sampled displacement correlation).

Register golden baselines at parameter extremes for all four primitive families (min/max wobble amplitude and frequency, min/max stroke-width variation, zero/max texture opacity and scale, paint-reveal at t=0/0.5/1.0, glyph-stroke at small and large point sizes) through the golden-image-harness registry, and prove they compare clean.
